How's that all working out for you?' | ROW-637: 'And you know that you were supposed to make that payment Friday. If you didn't it just comes out of your paycheck' | ROW-638: 'I just have to file the paperwork to do that' | ROW-639: 'You'd rather let Olivia down than make Abby mad and that's pathetic' | ROW-645: 'My biggest issue is that you don't prioritize Olivia.' | ROW-646: 'We both know that you aren't taking her tonight because it would make Abby mad cause she claims you don't treat Milo like yours cause he's not' | ROW-648: 'You'd rather not hear her bitch so you are not going with Olivia' | ROW-652: 'She deserves a dad that prioritizes her but I know you won't' | ROW-653: 'You'll regret treating her poorly and not making the time and effort for her.' | ROW-654: 'You'll regret it one day but it will be too late' | ROW-656: 'I don't know how you live with yourself. Your daughter is begging you to go with her and you tell her no so you can go with another kid' | ROW-657: 'That is beyond fucked up'
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: Two RF-05 signal elements within the Oct 31 Halloween escalation cluster. SIGNAL 1 — ROW-637-638: Wage garnishment filing threat. Context: Melissa raised the child support payment mid-escalation ('you know that you were supposed to make that payment Friday. If you didn't it just comes out of your paycheck' / 'I just have to file the paperwork to do that'). Eli confirmed payment on Friday (ROW-640-647 confirms payment at 7:05pm Friday). The legal-filing threat was deployed BEFORE Melissa verified payment status, in the middle of an unrelated parenting dispute — functioning as additional pressure during the emotional escalation rather than as a genuine legal-status update. ADD-002 temporal rule: wage garnishment is a routine enforcement mechanism (not the custody/lawfare category that Eli identified as primary fear); classified Sev-1 (routine legal-mechanism deployment in escalation context). SIGNAL 2 — ROW-653-654: 'You'll regret treating her poorly and not making the time and effort for her. / You'll regret it one day but it will be too late.' Explicit implied-threat language; the taxonomy directly lists 'you'll regret this' as an RF-05 behavioral indicator. The framing asserts an inevitable future negative consequence for Eli's conduct — designed to create compliance pressure or punitive anticipation. The 'it will be too late' element adds temporal urgency. ADD-002: this type of implied consequence language is not custody-specific but creates a diffuse threat environment; Sev-1. Both signals operate within the same sustained escalation cluster and reinforce each other: the legal filing threat establishes a concrete compliance mechanism (I will act) while the 'you'll regret it' framing establishes an implied punitive future — together these constitute a mild intimidation pattern. Cross-ref RF-04: this cluster is ARC-RF04-001-S004 (first Sev-2 instance in RF-04 pass; full DARVO + contempt campaign). RF-05 signals are secondary elements within the larger RF-04 primary cluster.

peripheral_flags.note: ROW-619-659 Halloween escalation is ARC-RF04-001-S004 — full DARVO, sustained inadequate-father campaign, child-emotions weaponized, Abby-causation framing. RF-04 is primary. RF-05 signals (ROW-637-638 and ROW-653-654) are secondary elements within the RF-04 primary cluster.
ROW-636: 'house bitch and taxi' Abby-framework attack — RF-04 contempt + ADD-001 Abby-attack peripheral RF-05 (aggressive tone only; no legal/formal-reporting element; not RF-05 primary per ADD-001).

ROW-668-679: 'I've told you long ago that no calls after 8:30' / 'you need to respect boundaries' — RF-02 primary (phone blocking enforcement) + RF-05 peripheral (compliance-demand framing 'you've been told over and over'; no threat, consistent with B-001 ROW-124-125 pattern).

flagged_messages.context_notes: Trigger: Eli was working nights and there was a dispute about whether he would take Olivia trick-or-treating in her neighborhood or his (Milo also involved). Melissa escalated from the scheduling disagreement to a full inadequate-father campaign (ROW-619-659 = 40+ message sustained cluster). Eli confirmed he then drove to Melissa's neighborhood (ROW-662: 'came over. Drive through the and didn't see you guys') suggesting he did attempt to participate. The escalation partially defuses after ROW-660 when Eli asks if Olivia is still in costume — Melissa's responses turn logistical. RF-05 signals appear mid-escalation (ROW-638, legal filing) and at the emotional peak (ROW-653-654, regret framing) before de-escalation.
flagged_messages.quote_excerpt: 'You'll regret it one day but it will be too late' (ROW-654); 'I just have to file the paperwork to do that' (ROW-638)

unflagged_notable.reason_noted: Near-boundary RF-05: 'Stop calling while you're at the store and half ass paying attention to her and talking to the clerk' — directive/contemptuous but not threatening. RF-04 contempt. Excluded from RF-05.

arcs_promoted_this_batch: ARC-RF05-001 initialized as CANDIDATE — first RF-05 primary signal: implied threat ('you'll regret it') + legal-filing deployment in escalation context. Single episode; threshold for WATCHING = second distinct episode primary signal.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: B-002 (Oct-Nov 2022) introduces the first RF-05 primary signals in the dataset — both within the Oct 31 Halloween escalation cluster. The rest of the batch is cooperative-to-neutral logistics. The Halloween cluster (ROW-619-659) is a sustained multi-vector attack with RF-04 as primary but RF-05 implied-threat and legal-threat elements. The 'you'll regret it' framing (ROW-653-654) is the first explicit implied-threat language in the record.
batch_observations.cross_rf_context: RF-04 B-002 (ARC-RF04-001-S004): Oct 31 Halloween cluster is the RF-04 pass's first Sev-2 event and the signal that promoted ARC-RF04-001 from WATCHING to ACTIVE. The RF-05 signals are embedded within the RF-04 primary cluster — the intimidation elements augment but do not dominate the manipulation pattern at this stage.
